page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7a.large is a compute-optimized virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7T83 processor running at a base frequency of 2.55 GHz. The instance is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying on network-attached storage and providing a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks reveal a divergence between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The AMD EPYC processor delivers strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, placing in the top 25%. However, multi-core CPU performance and overall PassMark CPU scores fall into the lower 25% tier. Memory bandwidth is a notable bottleneck, with cached small-block read speeds of 126.98 GB/sec and uncached large-block write speeds of 16.11 GB/sec, both ranking in the bottom 10% to 25% of tested hardware. Text processing and single-threaded compression tasks perform at an average level, while database operations, software compilation, and static web serving throughput remain in the bottom 10%.

The ecs.c7a.large offers a cost-efficient entry point for dedicated compute resources without the overhead of shared-core CPU credit systems. The primary tradeoff is the limited 4.0 GB memory capacity and low memory bandwidth, which constrains its ability to handle memory-intensive applications or concurrent multi-threaded workloads. This instance is best suited for lightweight, single-threaded applications, basic web serving, simple text processing, and small-scale development or testing environments that require dedicated CPU allocation but do not demand high memory throughput.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7a.large is a compute-optimized virtual server featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs and 4.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7T83 processor at 2.55 GHz on the KVM hypervisor. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, utilizing a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates strong single-core CPU performance, though multi-core execution and memory bandwidth are relatively weak, with uncached write speeds at 16.11 GB/sec. The instance's 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it a cost-efficient option for dedicated compute tasks that do not require extensive memory. It is best suited for lightweight single-threaded applications, basic text processing, and small-scale development environments.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7a.large is a compute-optimized virtual server with 2 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 4.0 GB of RAM, and 1 Gbps network bandwidth, suitable for lightweight single-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7a.large features 2 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 4.0 GB of RAM, offering strong single-core performance for light workloads.
tagline: A dedicated dual-core compute instance featuring strong single-threaded performance and a compact memory footprint for lightweight workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 2 dedicated AMD EPYC 7T83 vCPUs at 2.55 GHz
Equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory and 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Delivers strong single-core CPU performance in benchmark testing
Lacks local storage and GPU acceleration
Best suited for lightweight single-threaded applications and development environments
